title: Pulmonary Diagnostics II
description: Arterial and capillary blood gas sampling, acid-base and gas exchange, ABG analyzers, and pulmonary imaging (chest x-ray, CT, MRI, ultrasound, V/Q, PET).

lessons.title: Arterial & Capillary Blood Gas Sampling
lessons.description: Objective 1.1.1 — ABG indications, contraindications, hazards, and sample sites; equipment selection; Modified Allen test; radial, brachial, and femoral puncture; indwelling arterial lines (A-lines) including dampening and transducer position; and capillary blood gas procedure.

lessons.title: Acid–Base Homeostasis
lessons.description: Objective 1.2.1 — pH and [H⁺] chemistry; pH homeostasis via lungs, kidneys, and blood buffers; CO₂ production, transport, and excretion; energy metabolism and the respiratory quotient (RQ); the oxyhemoglobin dissociation curve and its modifiers (pH, temperature, 2,3-DPG, Bohr/Haldane effects); and step-by-step ABG interpretation using ROME.

lessons.title: Evaluating Oxygenation, Blood Gas Analyzers & QA/QC
lessons.description: Objective 1.3.1 — Hypoxemia vs. hypoxia and PaO₂ staging (with age adjustment); arterial oxygen content (CaO₂); the alveolar air equation and A–a gradient; SANZ/Severinghaus/Clark electrodes; co-oximetry and the four hemoglobin species (HbO₂, HbCO, MetHb, SHb); point-of-care testing (i-STAT); regulatory agencies (CLIA, CAP, TJC, CLIP, CMS, NCCLS); calibration (1-, 2-, 3-point); and Levey-Jennings / Westgard quality-control rules.

lessons.title: Radiographic Imaging & Chest X-ray Interpretation
lessons.description: Objective 1.4.1 — Chest X-ray overview, indications, positions/projections (PA, AP, lateral neck, lateral decubitus), quality evaluation (penetration, rotation, inspiration), anatomical landmarks, and tube/catheter placement verification.

lessons.title: Abnormal Chest X-ray & Advanced Imaging Interpretation
lessons.description: Objective 1.5.1 — Identify abnormal chest X-ray findings across common cardiopulmonary pathologies (COPD, pneumonia, atelectasis, pleural effusion, pneumothorax, hemothorax, left heart failure, PE, ARDS, epiglottitis, croup, foreign body aspiration, flail chest) and describe the clinical utility of advanced imaging modalities (CT, MRI, ultrasound, V/Q scan, PET).

lessons.title: Module Exam
lessons.description: Comprehensive 50-question module exam covering ABG sampling, acid-base homeostasis, oxygenation evaluation, blood gas analyzers, chest X-ray interpretation, advanced pulmonary imaging, and quality control.

audio.title: The Physics of Arterial Blood Gas Sampling
The Invisible Tightrope of Blood pH
The Invisible Math of Clinical Oxygenation
The Physics of Clinical Chest X-Rays

resources.title: Arterial Line Insertion (video)
resources.description: Demonstration of an indwelling arterial line (A-line) insertion procedure.

resources.title: Drawing Blood from an Arterial Line (video)
resources.description: Three-way stopcock sampling technique for drawing arterial blood from an A-line. Start at 3:17.

resources.title: Acid–Base Homeostasis Review (video)
resources.description: Companion video for Lesson 2 covering the chemistry of pH, the hydrolysis equation, and the lung/kidney/buffer regulation of acid–base balance.
